behavior_in_task_description.reason: Instruction names router.forward, gate module, router_gating_linear, nn.Linear subclass with required __init__ params (in_features, out_features, router_dtype), state_dict compatibility, and ruff 0.14.9 version — all test assertions map to these.

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction describes required behavior (gate as module, subclass requirements, delegation to router_gating_linear) without dictating specific line changes or exact class name.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests use AST to find any nn.Linear subclass (not a specific name like RouterGateLinear) and check behavioral equivalence; the subclass name requirement is stated in instruction as an nn.Linear subclass with required __init__ signature.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Tests instantiate the router, register hooks, call router(x), and verify hook firing counts and outputs — real execution.

test_not_tautological.reason: Stub forward would not produce matching router_gating_linear output and hook-fire tests require actual module invocation; tests check hook_count==1 after real call.

behavior_in_tests.reason: Hook firing, subclass requirement, router_gating_linear delegation, ruff checks, and state_dict compat are all tested per the instruction requirements.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are organized with section comments (Static, Fail-to-pass) and descriptive docstrings/names.

structured_data_schema.reason: Task is code behavior fix, no structured data output.

no_typos.reason: Paths and names are consistent across instruction and tests.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Instruction references 'the MoE router' generally; doesn't pin exact line numbers, though localization is somewhat easy given the domain.
